# Repository Request

> **How to use this template**
>
> 1. Add a YAML config file under `repos/` (copy `repos/_template.yml`).
> 2. Fill in all sections below.
> 3. Assign a reviewer from the `org-admins` team.
> 4. Merging this PR will automatically create and onboard the repository.

---

## Repository Details

| Field | Value |
| ----- | ----- |
| **Name** | <!-- e.g. svc-payments-api --> |
| **Type** | <!-- svc \| lib \| infra \| sandbox --> |
| **Owning Team** | <!-- e.g. backend, platform, frontend --> |
| **Visibility** | <!-- private \| internal \| public --> |
| **Description** | <!-- one-line description --> |

## Purpose

<!-- Why is this repository needed? What problem does it solve? -->

## Checklist

- [ ] File name in `repos/` matches the `name` field in the YAML.
- [ ] Repository name follows the naming convention in `repos/README.md`.
- [ ] Owning team exists in the GitHub organization.
- [ ] Description is clear and concise (one sentence).
- [ ] Visibility is set to `private` unless a business justification applies.
- [ ] Topics are lowercase and use hyphens only.
- [ ] I have read `docs/pipeline/repo-onboarding.md`.

## Exception / Notes

<!-- Leave empty if no exceptions apply. -->
